entries.label: Plugin Config
entries.help: Plugin-defined configuration payload interpreted by that plugin's own schema and validation rules. Use only documented fields from the plugin to prevent ignored or invalid settings.

entries.label: Plugin Enabled
entries.help: Per-plugin enablement override for a specific entry, applied on top of global plugin policy (restart required). Use this to stage plugin rollout gradually across environments.

entries.label: Plugin Hook Policy
entries.help: Per-plugin typed hook policy controls for core-enforced safety gates. Use this to constrain high-impact hook categories without disabling the entire plugin.

entries.label: Allow Conversation Access Hooks
entries.help: Controls whether this plugin may read raw conversation content from typed hooks such as `before_agent_run`, `before_model_resolve`, `before_agent_reply`, `llm_input`, `llm_output`, `before_agent_finalize`, and `agent_end`. Non-bundled plugins must opt in explicitly.

entries.label: Allow Prompt Injection Hooks
entries.help: Controls whether this plugin may mutate prompts through typed hooks. Set false to block `before_prompt_build` and ignore prompt-mutating fields from legacy `before_agent_start`, while preserving legacy `modelOverride` and `providerOverride` behavior.

entries.label: Plugin Hook Timeout (ms)
entries.help: Default timeout in milliseconds for this plugin's typed hooks, capped at 600000. Use this to bound slow plugin hooks without changing plugin code; per-hook values in hooks.timeouts take precedence.

entries.label: Plugin Hook Timeout Overrides
entries.help: Per-hook timeout overrides in milliseconds keyed by typed hook name, capped at 600000. Use narrow overrides for known slow hooks such as before_prompt_build or agent_end instead of raising every hook timeout.

entries.label: Plugin LLM Policy
entries.help: Per-plugin api.runtime.llm.complete controls for model and agent override trust. Keep this unset unless a plugin must explicitly steer host-owned completion calls.

entries.label: Allow Plugin LLM Agent Override
entries.help: Explicitly allows this plugin to request api.runtime.llm.complete against a non-default agent id. Keep false unless the plugin is trusted for cross-agent model access.

entries.label: Plugin LLM Allowed Models
entries.help: Allowed override targets for trusted plugin LLM completions as canonical "provider/model" refs. Use "*" only when you intentionally allow any model.

entries.label: Allow Plugin LLM Model Override
entries.help: Explicitly allows this plugin to request model overrides in api.runtime.llm.complete. Keep false unless the plugin is trusted to steer model selection.

entries.label: Plugin Subagent Policy
entries.help: Per-plugin subagent runtime controls for model override trust and allowlists. Keep this unset unless a plugin must explicitly steer subagent model selection.

entries.label: Plugin Subagent Allowed Models
entries.help: Allowed override targets for trusted plugin subagent runs as canonical "provider/model" refs. Use "*" only when you intentionally allow any model.

entries.label: Allow Plugin Subagent Model Override
entries.help: Explicitly allows this plugin to request provider/model overrides in background subagent runs. Keep false unless the plugin is trusted to steer model selection.

entries.label: ACPX Runtime
entries.help: Embedded ACP runtime backend with plugin-owned session and transport management. (plugin: acpx)

entries.label: ACPX Runtime Config
entries.help: Plugin-defined config payload for acpx.

entries.label: Agent Commands
entries.help: Optional per-agent command overrides for the embedded ACP runtime.

entries.label: Default Working Directory
entries.help: Default working directory for embedded ACP session operations when not set per session.

entries.label: MCP Servers
entries.help: Named MCP server definitions to inject into embedded ACP session bootstrap. Each entry needs a command and can include args and env.

entries.label: Non-Interactive Permission Policy
entries.help: Policy when interactive permission prompts are unavailable.

entries.label: OpenClaw Tools MCP Bridge
entries.help: Default off. When enabled, inject the built-in OpenClaw core-tools MCP server into embedded ACP sessions so ACP agents can call selected built-in tools such as cron.

entries.label: Permission Mode
entries.help: Default permission policy for embedded ACP runtime prompts.

entries.label: Plugin Tools MCP Bridge
entries.help: Default off. When enabled, inject the built-in OpenClaw plugin-tools MCP server into embedded ACP sessions so ACP agents can call plugin-registered tools.

entries.label: Health Probe Agent
entries.help: Agent id used for the embedded ACP runtime health probe. Defaults to the first `acp.allowedAgents` entry when that allowlist is set, otherwise to the runtime built-in probe agent (codex). Set this explicitly (for example `opencode` or `claude`) when the default probe agent is not installed or not authenticated, so the whole embedded ACP backend does not get marked unavailable.

entries.label: Queue Owner TTL Seconds
entries.help: Reserved compatibility field for the older embedded ACPX queue-owner path. Accepted for compatibility and logged as ignored.

entries.label: State Directory
entries.help: Directory used for embedded ACP session state and persistence.

entries.label: Strict Windows cmd Wrapper
entries.help: Legacy compatibility field. The current embedded acpx/runtime package uses its own Windows command resolution behavior. Setting this to false is accepted for compatibility and logged as ignored.

entries.label: Prompt Timeout Seconds
entries.help: Timeout for each embedded runtime turn. Defaults to 120 seconds so slower Gemini CLI ACP startups have room to initialize.

entries.label: Active Memory
entries.help: Runs a bounded blocking memory sub-agent before eligible conversational replies and injects relevant memory into prompt context. (plugin: active-memory)

entries.label: Active Memory Config
entries.help: Plugin-defined config payload for active-memory.

entries.label: Target Agents
entries.help: Explicit agent ids that may use active memory.

entries.label: Allowed Chat IDs
entries.help: Optional explicit allowlist of chat/user IDs (e.g. Feishu chat_id oc_xxx, open_id ou_xxx, Telegram chat id, Slack channel id). When non-empty, Active Memory only runs for sessions whose conversation id is in the list, across **every** chat type at once (direct, group, channel). Setting this narrows every allowed chat type simultaneously — if you want 'all directs + only specific groups', use allowedChatTypes: ['group'] + allowedChatIds: [<group ids>] and rely on direct chats being matched via the direct session id (e.g. the user's open_id) instead. Leave empty to fall back to allowedChatTypes alone.

entries.label: Allowed Chat Types
entries.help: Choose which session types may run Active Memory. Defaults to direct-message style sessions only, but explicit portal/webchat sessions can also be enabled.

entries.label: Circuit Breaker Cooldown (ms)
entries.help: How long to skip recall after the circuit breaker trips, in milliseconds. Default: 60000 (1 minute).

entries.label: Circuit Breaker Max Timeouts
entries.help: Skip recall after this many consecutive timeouts for the same agent/model. Resets on a successful recall or after the cooldown expires. Default: 3.

entries.label: Denied Chat IDs
entries.help: Optional explicit denylist of chat/user IDs. Sessions whose resolved conversation id matches the list are skipped even when the chat type is allowed. Applied after allowedChatIds.

entries.label: Active Memory Recall
entries.help: Globally enable or pause Active Memory recall while keeping the plugin command available.

entries.label: Enable Logging
entries.help: Emit active memory timing and result logs.

entries.label: Max Summary Characters
entries.help: Maximum total characters allowed in the active-memory summary.

entries.label: Memory Model
entries.help: Provider/model used for the blocking memory sub-agent.

entries.label: Fallback Memory Model
entries.help: Optional provider/model to use if no explicit plugin model, session model, or agent primary model resolves.

entries.label: Model Fallback Policy
entries.help: Deprecated compatibility field. modelFallback is only the chain-resolution last resort when no explicit plugin model, session model, or agent primary model resolves; it is not runtime failover.

entries.label: Persist Transcripts
entries.help: Keep blocking memory sub-agent session transcripts on disk in a separate plugin-owned directory.

entries.label: Prompt Append
entries.help: Advanced: append extra operator instructions after the default Active Memory sub-agent instructions.

entries.label: Prompt Override
entries.help: Advanced: replace the default Active Memory sub-agent instructions. Conversation context is still appended.

entries.label: Prompt Style
entries.help: Choose how eager or strict the blocking memory sub-agent should be when deciding whether to return memory.

entries.label: QMD Search Mode
entries.help: Override the QMD search mode used by the blocking memory sub-agent. Defaults to fast lexical search; use inherit to match the main memory backend setting.

entries.label: Query Mode
entries.help: Choose whether the blocking memory sub-agent sees only the latest user message, a small recent tail, or the full conversation.

entries.label: Setup Grace Timeout (ms)
entries.help: Advanced: extra blocking budget for cold embedded-run setup before the recall timeout is considered exhausted. Defaults to 0 so timeoutMs remains the main-lane hook budget unless you opt in.

entries.label: Thinking Override
entries.help: Advanced: optional thinking level for the blocking memory sub-agent. Defaults to off for speed.

entries.label: Allowed Memory Tools
entries.help: Advanced: tool names the blocking memory sub-agent may use. Defaults to memory_search and memory_get, or memory_recall when plugins.slots.memory selects memory-lancedb; configure this for other non-core memory providers. Wildcards, group entries, and core agent tools are ignored.

entries.label: Transcript Directory
entries.help: Relative directory under the agent sessions folder used when transcript persistence is enabled.

entries.help: Controls whether this plugin may mutate prompts through typed hooks. Set false to block `before_prompt_build` and ignore prompt-mutating fields from legacy `before_agent_start`, while preserving legacy `modelOverride` and `providerOverride` behavior.

entries.label: Plugin Hook Timeout (ms)
entries.help: Default timeout in milliseconds for this plugin's typed hooks, capped at 600000. Use this to bound slow plugin hooks without changing plugin code; per-hook values in hooks.timeouts take precedence.

entries.label: Plugin Hook Timeout Overrides
entries.help: Per-hook timeout overrides in milliseconds keyed by typed hook name, capped at 600000. Use narrow overrides for known slow hooks such as before_prompt_build or agent_end instead of raising every hook timeout.

entries.label: Codex
entries.help: Codex app-server harness and Codex-managed GPT model catalog. (plugin: codex)

entries.label: Codex Config
entries.help: Plugin-defined config payload for codex.

entries.label: App Server
entries.help: Runtime controls for connecting to Codex app-server.

entries.label: Approval Policy
entries.help: Codex native approval policy sent to thread start, resume, and turns.

entries.label: Approvals Reviewer
entries.help: Use user approvals or Codex auto_review for native app-server approvals. guardian_subagent remains accepted for compatibility.

entries.label: Arguments
entries.help: Arguments used for stdio transport. Defaults to app-server --listen stdio://.

entries.label: Auth Token
entries.help: Bearer token sent to the WebSocket app-server.

entries.label: Clear Environment
entries.help: Environment variable names removed from the spawned stdio app-server process after overrides are applied.

entries.label: Command
entries.help: Executable used for stdio transport. Leave unset to use OpenClaw's managed Codex binary.

entries.label: Default Workspace
entries.help: Workspace used by /codex bind when --cwd is omitted.

entries.label: Headers
entries.help: Additional headers sent to the WebSocket app-server.

entries.label: Execution Mode
entries.help: Use yolo for unchained local execution or guardian for Codex guardian-reviewed approvals.

entries.label: Request Timeout
entries.help: Maximum time to wait for Codex app-server control-plane requests.

entries.label: Sandbox
entries.help: Codex native sandbox mode sent to thread start and resume.

entries.label: Service Tier
entries.help: Optional Codex app-server service tier. Use priority, flex, or null. Legacy fast is accepted as priority.

entries.label: Transport
entries.help: Use stdio to spawn Codex locally, or websocket to connect to an already-running app-server.

entries.label: Turn Completion Idle Timeout
entries.help: Maximum quiet time after Codex accepts a turn or after a turn-scoped app-server request before OpenClaw interrupts the turn while waiting for turn/completed.

entries.label: WebSocket URL
entries.help: Codex app-server WebSocket URL when transport is websocket.

entries.label: Dynamic Tool Excludes
entries.help: Additional OpenClaw dynamic tool names to omit from Codex app-server turns.

entries.label: Dynamic Tools Loading
entries.help: Use searchable to defer OpenClaw dynamic tools behind Codex tool search, or direct to expose them in the initial context.

entries.label: Native Codex Plugins
entries.help: Controls native Codex plugin availability for Codex harness turns.

entries.label: Allow Destructive Plugin Actions
entries.help: Default policy for plugin app write or destructive action elicitations. Defaults to true.

entries.label: Enable Native Plugins
entries.help: Expose explicit migrated Codex plugin entries to Codex harness turns.

entries.label: Migrated Plugin Entries
entries.help: Explicit migration-authored plugin entries. The wildcard key * is not supported.

entries.label: Computer Use
entries.help: Controls Codex app-server setup for the Computer Use plugin.

entries.label: Auto Install
entries.help: Install the configured Computer Use plugin when Codex-mode turns start.

entries.label: Enable Computer Use
entries.help: When true, Codex-mode turns require the configured Computer Use MCP server to be available.

entries.label: Marketplace Discovery Timeout
entries.help: Maximum time to wait for Codex app-server to finish loading marketplaces during Computer Use install.

entries.label: Marketplace Name
entries.help: Optional registered Codex marketplace name containing the Computer Use plugin.

entries.label: Marketplace Path
entries.help: Optional local Codex marketplace file path containing the Computer Use plugin.

entries.label: Marketplace Source
entries.help: Optional Codex marketplace source to add before installing Computer Use.

entries.label: MCP Server Name
entries.help: MCP server name exposed by the Computer Use plugin.

entries.label: Plugin Name
entries.help: Codex marketplace plugin name for Computer Use.

entries.label: Model Discovery
entries.help: Plugin-owned controls for discovering Codex app-server models.

entries.label: Enable Discovery
entries.help: When false, OpenClaw keeps the Codex harness available but uses the bundled fallback model list.

entries.label: Discovery Timeout
entries.help: Maximum time to wait for Codex app-server model discovery before falling back to the bundled model list.

entries.label: Plugin Hook Policy
entries.help: Per-plugin typed hook policy controls for core-enforced safety gates. Use this to constrain high-impact hook categories without disabling the entire plugin.

entries.label: Allow Conversation Access Hooks
entries.help: Controls whether this plugin may read raw conversation content from typed hooks such as `before_agent_run`, `before_model_resolve`, `before_agent_reply`, `llm_input`, `llm_output`, `before_agent_finalize`, and `agent_end`. Non-bundled plugins must opt in explicitly.

entries.label: Allow Prompt Injection Hooks
entries.help: Controls whether this plugin may mutate prompts through typed hooks. Set false to block `before_prompt_build` and ignore prompt-mutating fields from legacy `before_agent_start`, while preserving legacy `modelOverride` and `providerOverride` behavior.

entries.label: Plugin Hook Timeout (ms)
entries.help: Default timeout in milliseconds for this plugin's typed hooks, capped at 600000. Use this to bound slow plugin hooks without changing plugin code; per-hook values in hooks.timeouts take precedence.

entries.label: Plugin Hook Timeout Overrides
entries.help: Per-hook timeout overrides in milliseconds keyed by typed hook name, capped at 600000. Use narrow overrides for known slow hooks such as before_prompt_build or agent_end instead of raising every hook timeout.

entries.label: Plugin LLM Policy
entries.help: Per-plugin api.runtime.llm.complete controls for model and agent override trust. Keep this unset unless a plugin must explicitly steer host-owned completion calls.

entries.label: Allow Plugin LLM Agent Override
entries.help: Explicitly allows this plugin to request api.runtime.llm.complete against a non-default agent id. Keep false unless the plugin is trusted for cross-agent model access.

entries.label: Plugin LLM Allowed Models
entries.help: Allowed override targets for trusted plugin LLM completions as canonical "provider/model" refs. Use "*" only when you intentionally allow any model.

entries.label: Allow Plugin LLM Model Override
entries.help: Explicitly allows this plugin to request model overrides in api.runtime.llm.complete. Keep false unless the plugin is trusted to steer model selection.

entries.label: Plugin Subagent Policy
entries.help: Per-plugin subagent runtime controls for model override trust and allowlists. Keep this unset unless a plugin must explicitly steer subagent model selection.

entries.label: Plugin Subagent Allowed Models
entries.help: Allowed override targets for trusted plugin subagent runs as canonical "provider/model" refs. Use "*" only when you intentionally allow any model.

entries.label: Allow Plugin Subagent Model Override
entries.help: Explicitly allows this plugin to request provider/model overrides in background subagent runs. Keep false unless the plugin is trusted to steer model selection.

entries.label: Google Meet
entries.help: Join Google Meet calls through Chrome or Twilio transports. (plugin: google-meet)

entries.label: Google Meet Config
entries.help: Plugin-defined config payload for google-meet.

entries.label: Chrome Audio Backend
entries.help: BlackHole 2ch is required for local duplex audio routing.

entries.label: Audio Buffer Bytes
entries.help: SoX processing buffer for generated Chrome command-pair audio commands. Lower values reduce latency but may underrun on busy hosts.

entries.label: Audio Format
entries.help: Command-pair audio format. PCM16 24 kHz is the default Chrome/Meet path; G.711 mu-law 8 kHz remains available for legacy command pairs.

entries.label: Audio Input Command
entries.help: Command that writes meeting audio to stdout in chrome.audioFormat.

entries.label: Audio Output Command
entries.help: Command that reads assistant audio from stdin in chrome.audioFormat.

entries.label: Auto Join Guest Screen
entries.help: Best-effort guest-name fill and Join Now click through OpenClaw browser automation.

entries.label: Barge-In Cooldown (ms)
entries.help: Minimum delay between repeated barge-in clears.

entries.label: Barge-In Input Command
entries.help: Optional Gateway-hosted microphone command that writes signed 16-bit little-endian mono PCM for human interruption detection while assistant playback is active.

entries.label: Barge-In Peak Threshold
entries.help: Peak level on chrome.bargeInInputCommand that counts as a human interruption.

entries.label: Barge-In RMS Threshold
entries.help: RMS level on chrome.bargeInInputCommand that counts as a human interruption.

entries.label: Guest Name
entries.help: Used when Chrome lands on the signed-out Meet guest-name screen.

entries.label: Reuse Existing Meet Tab
entries.help: Avoids opening duplicate tabs for the same Meet URL.

entries.label: Wait For In-Call (ms)
entries.help: Waits for Chrome to report that the Meet tab is in-call before the realtime intro speaks.

entries.label: Chrome Node
entries.help: Node id/name/IP that owns Chrome, BlackHole, and SoX for chrome-node transport.

entries.label: Default Mode
entries.help: Agent uses realtime transcription plus regular OpenClaw TTS. Bidi uses the realtime voice model directly. Transcribe observes only.

entries.label: Default Meeting
entries.help: Meet URL, meeting code, or spaces/{id} used when commands omit a meeting.

entries.label: Default Transport
entries.help: Chrome uses a signed-in browser profile. Chrome-node runs Chrome on a paired node. Twilio uses Meet dial-in numbers.

entries.label: Cached Access Token Expiry
entries.help: Unix epoch milliseconds used only for the cached access-token fast path.

entries.label: Preview Acknowledged
entries.help: Confirms you understand the Google Meet Media API is still Developer Preview.

entries.label: Realtime Consult Agent
entries.help: OpenClaw agent id used by openclaw_agent_consult. Defaults to "main".

entries.defaultValue: You are joining a private Google Meet as an OpenClaw voice transport. Keep spoken replies brief and natural. In agent mode, wait for OpenClaw consult results and speak them exactly. In bidi mode, answer directly and call openclaw_agent_consult for deeper reasoning, current information, or tools.
entries.label: Realtime Instructions

entries.defaultValue: Say exactly: I'm here and listening.
entries.label: Realtime Intro Message
entries.help: Spoken once when the realtime bridge is ready. Set to an empty string to join silently.

entries.label: Bidi Realtime Model
entries.help: Only used by mode=bidi. Agent mode answers with the configured OpenClaw agent and regular TTS.

entries.label: Speech Provider
entries.help: Compatibility fallback for both realtime transcription and bidi voice. Prefer realtime.transcriptionProvider and realtime.voiceProvider for new configs.

entries.label: Realtime Strategy
entries.help: Legacy realtime alias setting. Use mode=agent or mode=bidi for new Meet joins.

entries.label: Realtime Tool Policy
entries.help: Safe read-only tools are available by default; owner requests can unlock broader tools.

entries.label: Realtime Transcription Provider
entries.help: Agent mode uses this provider to transcribe meeting audio before regular OpenClaw TTS answers.

entries.label: Bidi Voice Provider
entries.help: Bidi mode uses this realtime voice provider. Falls back to realtime.provider when unset.

entries.label: Legacy DTMF Delay (ms)
entries.help: Compatibility setting from the old post-connect DTMF flow. Twilio Meet joins now play DTMF before realtime connect.

entries.label: Legacy Post-DTMF Speech Delay (ms)
entries.help: Compatibility setting from the old delayed-speech flow. Twilio Meet joins now carry the intro as the initial Voice Call message.

entries.help: Per-plugin typed hook policy controls for core-enforced safety gates. Use this to constrain high-impact hook categories without disabling the entire plugin.

entries.label: Allow Conversation Access Hooks
entries.help: Controls whether this plugin may read raw conversation content from typed hooks such as `before_agent_run`, `before_model_resolve`, `before_agent_reply`, `llm_input`, `llm_output`, `before_agent_finalize`, and `agent_end`. Non-bundled plugins must opt in explicitly.

entries.label: Allow Prompt Injection Hooks
entries.help: Controls whether this plugin may mutate prompts through typed hooks. Set false to block `before_prompt_build` and ignore prompt-mutating fields from legacy `before_agent_start`, while preserving legacy `modelOverride` and `providerOverride` behavior.

entries.label: Plugin Hook Timeout (ms)
entries.help: Default timeout in milliseconds for this plugin's typed hooks, capped at 600000. Use this to bound slow plugin hooks without changing plugin code; per-hook values in hooks.timeouts take precedence.

entries.label: Plugin Hook Timeout Overrides
entries.help: Per-hook timeout overrides in milliseconds keyed by typed hook name, capped at 600000. Use narrow overrides for known slow hooks such as before_prompt_build or agent_end instead of raising every hook timeout.

entries.label: Plugin LLM Policy
entries.help: Per-plugin api.runtime.llm.complete controls for model and agent override trust. Keep this unset unless a plugin must explicitly steer host-owned completion calls.

entries.label: Allow Plugin LLM Agent Override
entries.help: Explicitly allows this plugin to request api.runtime.llm.complete against a non-default agent id. Keep false unless the plugin is trusted for cross-agent model access.

entries.label: Plugin LLM Allowed Models
entries.help: Allowed override targets for trusted plugin LLM completions as canonical "provider/model" refs. Use "*" only when you intentionally allow any model.

entries.label: Allow Plugin LLM Model Override
entries.help: Explicitly allows this plugin to request model overrides in api.runtime.llm.complete. Keep false unless the plugin is trusted to steer model selection.

entries.label: Plugin Subagent Policy
entries.help: Per-plugin subagent runtime controls for model override trust and allowlists. Keep this unset unless a plugin must explicitly steer subagent model selection.

entries.label: Plugin Subagent Allowed Models
entries.help: Allowed override targets for trusted plugin subagent runs as canonical "provider/model" refs. Use "*" only when you intentionally allow any model.

entries.label: Allow Plugin Subagent Model Override
entries.help: Explicitly allows this plugin to request provider/model overrides in background subagent runs. Keep false unless the plugin is trusted to steer model selection.

entries.help: Controls whether this plugin may read raw conversation content from typed hooks such as `before_agent_run`, `before_model_resolve`, `before_agent_reply`, `llm_input`, `llm_output`, `before_agent_finalize`, and `agent_end`. Non-bundled plugins must opt in explicitly.

entries.label: Allow Prompt Injection Hooks
entries.help: Controls whether this plugin may mutate prompts through typed hooks. Set false to block `before_prompt_build` and ignore prompt-mutating fields from legacy `before_agent_start`, while preserving legacy `modelOverride` and `providerOverride` behavior.

entries.label: Plugin Hook Timeout (ms)
entries.help: Default timeout in milliseconds for this plugin's typed hooks, capped at 600000. Use this to bound slow plugin hooks without changing plugin code; per-hook values in hooks.timeouts take precedence.

entries.label: Plugin Hook Timeout Overrides
entries.help: Per-hook timeout overrides in milliseconds keyed by typed hook name, capped at 600000. Use narrow overrides for known slow hooks such as before_prompt_build or agent_end instead of raising every hook timeout.

entries.label: Plugin LLM Policy
entries.help: Per-plugin api.runtime.llm.complete controls for model and agent override trust. Keep this unset unless a plugin must explicitly steer host-owned completion calls.

entries.label: Allow Plugin LLM Agent Override
entries.help: Explicitly allows this plugin to request api.runtime.llm.complete against a non-default agent id. Keep false unless the plugin is trusted for cross-agent model access.

entries.label: Plugin LLM Allowed Models
entries.help: Allowed override targets for trusted plugin LLM completions as canonical "provider/model" refs. Use "*" only when you intentionally allow any model.

entries.label: Allow Plugin LLM Model Override
entries.help: Explicitly allows this plugin to request model overrides in api.runtime.llm.complete. Keep false unless the plugin is trusted to steer model selection.

entries.label: Plugin Subagent Policy
entries.help: Per-plugin subagent runtime controls for model override trust and allowlists. Keep this unset unless a plugin must explicitly steer subagent model selection.

entries.label: Plugin Subagent Allowed Models
entries.help: Allowed override targets for trusted plugin subagent runs as canonical "provider/model" refs. Use "*" only when you intentionally allow any model.

entries.label: Allow Plugin Subagent Model Override
entries.help: Explicitly allows this plugin to request provider/model overrides in background subagent runs. Keep false unless the plugin is trusted to steer model selection.
